name: a rule absent from a fresh fetch is marked deprecated:true with deprecated_at + deprecated_reason removed-upstream (no silent drop)
command:
OUT=/tmp/cl564-r10-$$; mkdir -p $OUT/out && cat > $OUT/v1.html <<'HTML'

Rule alpha

alpha.

Rule beta

beta.

HTML cat > $OUT/v2.html <<'HTML'

Rule alpha

alpha.

HTML cat > $OUT/reg1.yaml < $OUT/reg2.yaml </dev/null; bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/standards-refresh.sh" --registry $OUT/reg2.yaml --force --now 2026-08-15T00:00:00Z --out-dir $OUT/out 2>e.err; grep -q 'deprecated=1' e.err && grep -q 'deprecated_reason: removed-upstream' $OUT/out/european-union/r10-src.yaml && echo deprecated-at-on-removal 1>&2; rm -rf $OUT
